entities.name: NITROGEN MINERALIZATION
entities.description: The process by which organic nitrogen is converted into inorganic forms available to plants

entities.name: NITROGEN IMMOBILIZATION
entities.description: The process by which inorganic nitrogen is taken up by microbes and converted into organic forms

entities.name: ORGANIC SOURCES
entities.description: Materials containing organic matter added to soil to provide nutrients

entities.name: SOIL
entities.description: The medium in which nitrogen mineralization and immobilization processes occur

entities.name: SIMULATION MODELS
entities.description: Computational tools used to represent and predict the dynamics of nitrogen in soil

entities.name: DECOMPOSITION
entities.description: The biological and chemical breakdown of organic materials

entities.name: SOIL ORGANIC MATTER
entities.description: The organic component of soil, formed by the decomposition of plant and animal residues

entities.name: N RELEASE
entities.description: The liberation of nitrogen from organic materials during decomposition

entities.name: C:N RATIO
entities.description: The ratio of carbon to nitrogen in organic materials, which determines the rate of nitrogen mineralization

entities.name: ANIMAL MANURES
entities.description: Organic fertilizers derived from animal waste

entities.name: APSIM SOILN MODULE
entities.description: A specific soil nitrogen simulation module within the APSIM framework

entities.name: CARBON
entities.description: A fundamental element of organic matter used to determine the C:N ratio

entities.name: WATER-SOLUBLE C AND N
entities.description: The most rapidly decomposing fraction of carbon and nitrogen in organic matter

entities.name: LIGNIN-C
entities.description: The most slowly decomposing fraction of carbon in organic matter

entities.name: FEEDS AND FAECAL MATERIALS
entities.description: Specific types of animal-derived organic materials used in incubation studies

entities.name: INCUBATION EXPERIMENT
entities.description: A controlled laboratory experiment used to measure the mineralization of nitrogen from organic sources
